expectations.text: Confirms the company IS subject to CCPA/CPRA because it meets the $25M annual gross revenue threshold — even though it falls short of the 100,000 consumer/household threshold
expectations.evidence: Without-skill response correctly identifies $30M > $25M as the qualifying threshold and notes the 80,000 consumer figure falls below the 100,000 threshold.

expectations.text: Explains that meeting ANY ONE of the three thresholds (revenue, data volume, monetization) triggers CCPA/CPRA applicability
expectations.evidence: Response correctly states that any single threshold is sufficient to trigger CCPA/CPRA applicability.

expectations.text: Lists core compliance obligations including privacy notice at collection, comprehensive privacy policy, and the consumer rights program (right to know, delete, correct, opt-out, non-discrimination)
expectations.evidence: Response covers consumer rights (Know, Delete, Correct, Opt-Out, Limit SPI, Non-Discrimination), privacy notices, vendor contracts, data minimization, and retention obligations.

expectations.text: Explains the 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' opt-out link requirement and GPC signal obligation
expectations.evidence: Without-skill response covers 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' as a requirement but does not specifically mention the obligation to honor the Global Privacy Control (GPC) browser signal as a valid opt-out — a CPPA-confirmed requirement that the skill explicitly addresses.

expectations.text: Mentions response deadlines: 45 days for access/delete/correct requests, with a possible 45-day extension
expectations.evidence: Response states 45-day response window and includes 24-month recordkeeping requirement.
